core_mechanism: recursive optimal-estimation filtering: sequentially fusing a model-based forecast with a new noisy observation, each weighted by the inverse of its dispersion, to minimize expected estimation error — generalized so that when the second moment diverges under heavy-tailed (power-law/Levy) noise a 'tail-covariance' replaces the covariance, turning the linear weighting update into a nonlinear matrix fixed-point equation
brief_reason: The abstract frames the method as combining forecasts and new observations into a minimum-error estimate (Kalman filtering) and generalizes this fusion to heavy-tailed noise by introducing a tail-covariance matrix that replaces the ill-defined covariance for tail exponents mu<=2, making the update nonlinear.
